segments.text: First listen and then answer the question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: What was wrong with the man's story?

translations.zh-Hans: 这个人的说法有什么问题？

segments.text: At the time the murder was committed, I was traveling on the 8 o'clock train to London, said the man.

translations.zh-Hans: “谋杀发生时，我正在开往伦敦的8点钟列车上。”那名男子说。

segments.text: Do you always catch such an early train?
Of course I do, answered the man.
I must be at work at 10 o'clock.
My employer will confirm that I was there on time.

translations.zh-Hans: “我的雇主可以证明我准时到了。”

segments.text: Would a later train get you to work on time?

translations.zh-Hans: “晚一点的火车能让你准时上班吗？”

segments.text: I suppose it would, but I never catch a later train.

translations.zh-Hans: “应该可以，不过我从不坐晚一点的火车。”

segments.text: At what time did you arrive at the station?
At 10 to 8, I bought a paper and waited for the train.

translations.zh-Hans: “7点50分。我买了份报纸，然后等车。”

segments.text: And you didn't notice anything unusual?
I suggest, said the inspector, that you are not telling the truth.

translations.zh-Hans: “我认为，”探长说，“你没有说实话。”

segments.text: I suggest that you did not catch the 8 o'clock train, but that you caught the 8.25, which would still get you to work on time.

translations.zh-Hans: “我认为你没有乘8点钟的火车，而是乘了8点25分那班；那班车仍能让你准时上班。”

segments.text: You see, on the morning of the murder, the 8 o'clock train did not run at all.

translations.zh-Hans: “你看，谋杀发生的那个早晨，8点钟的列车根本没有运行。”

segments.text: It broke down at Fern Green station and was taken off the line.

translations.zh-Hans: “它在芬格林车站发生故障，被调离了线路。”
